message:
chore: gitignore beads last-touched marker + swarm orchestrator scratch

Two recurring ephemeral patterns that keep appearing as untracked or
modified state between agent sessions:

- `.beads/last-touched` — beads writes this single-byte marker on
  every CLI invocation so `br sync` and `bv` can notice when issues
  have changed; pure session state, nothing to version.
- `.swarm/` and `.swarm_marching_orders.md` — the multi-agent swarm
  launcher drops its orchestrator directory (orchestrator.md,
  marching_orders.txt, ticks.log, review_orders.txt, phase.txt) and
  a root-level swarm briefing file; both are per-session coordination
  artifacts that don't belong in the repo.

`.beads/.br_history/`, `.beads/*.lock`, and `.ntm/` were already
covered above; this aligns the remaining gaps with the equivalent
entries in aadc, beads_rust, and remote_compilation_helper so
`ru status` stays clean across the fleet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -130,6 +130,13 @@ visible
 # Beads lock files (write/sync/bv/br advisory flocks)
 .beads/*.lock
 
+# Beads transient metadata
+.beads/last-touched
+
+# Multi-agent swarm coordination scratch state
+.swarm/
+.swarm_marching_orders.md
+
 # Ephemeral files (agent-generated, build artifacts, test detritus)
 ci-artifacts/
 proptest-regressions/
